title: What is Data?
concept: Data is recorded information — numbers, words, images, clicks. It's the raw material every AI learns from.

exercises.prompt: Recap: AI gets smart mainly by learning from…?
exercises.explain: Right — AI learns patterns from data. So the natural next question is: what exactly is data?

cards.title: You make data all day
cards.body: Every text you send, photo you snap, step your watch counts, song you play, map you search — you're generating data nonstop.

cards.title: So what is data?
cards.body: Data is recorded information: numbers, words, images, clicks, sensor readings — anything that can be stored and used later.

cards.title: Data is everywhere
cards.body: Your bank statement (numbers), tweets (text), Instagram (images), Spotify history (events), smartwatch heart-rate (sensor readings) — all data.

cards.title: Data is the fuel
cards.body: If AI is the engine, data is the fuel. No data → no learning. The more good data you have, the smarter AI can become.

cards.title: More isn't always better
cards.body: A mountain of messy or biased data can be worse than a little clean data. Quality beats raw quantity.

cards.title: Data vs Insight
cards.body: Data is raw; insight is what we pull out of it:

right.text:
The pattern:
"you walk more on weekends"

options.text: Recorded information — numbers, words, images, etc.

exercises.explain: Data is any recorded information — far broader than just spreadsheets.

exercises.prompt: Which one is an example of data?

options.text: The list of songs you played this week
A daydream you never wrote down

exercises.explain: If it's recorded and stored (like your play history), it's data. Unrecorded thoughts aren't.

exercises.prompt: True or false: AI can learn without any data.
exercises.explain: False — data is the fuel. No data means nothing to learn patterns from.

exercises.sentence: If AI is the engine, data is the ___.
exercises.explain: Data is the fuel — without it, the AI engine has nothing to run on.

exercises.prompt: A startup has a brilliant AI algorithm but almost no data. What's the likely problem?

options.text: Without enough good data, the AI can't learn much
Algorithms don't need data at all
Just add more code and it's fixed

exercises.explain: Exactly — even a great algorithm is starved without data. That's why data comes first in this course.
